pi_items.source: Cross-referenced setup_task.sh line 466 (cp command) and export_result.sh. Verified file exists with correct permissions (644, owned by ga) during live testing. File size was 15654 bytes in the test run.

Cross-referenced setup_task.sh line 467 (cp command). Verified file exists with correct permissions during live testing. Contains NSS Key Log Format entries for 7 TLS sessions (4 HTTPS servers x multiple requests).
Cross-referenced task description, export_result.sh line 14, and verifier.py. All three reference the same path. Verified during live testing that the ga user can write to ~/Documents/.

Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 44 (C2_SNI variable), /etc/hosts entry at line 112, and curl --resolve at line 312-313. The C2 HTTPS server binds to 10.0.1.20:443 with CN=secure-updates.corp-cdn.net. Verified via live tshark: 'tshark -r corporate_traffic.pcapng -Y tls.handshake.extensions_server_name -T fields -e tls.handshake.extensions_server_name' shows this SNI in Client Hello packets to 10.0.1.20.

The C2 server Python script (setup_task.sh lines 137-161) uses json.dumps() with a dict literal. Python json.dumps default formatting adds spaces after ':' and ','. Independently verified by running json.dumps({...}) locally. Also verified during live testing by running 'tshark -r corporate_traffic.pcapng -o tls.keylog_file:tls_keys.log -Y http.response -T fields -e http.file_data' which showed the decrypted HTTP response body matching this exact JSON string.

Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 43 (EXFIL_DOMAIN variable). Used in the DNS tunneling Python script (lines 352, 372). Also embedded in the C2 command JSON 'domain' field. Verified during live testing: tshark filter 'dns.qry.name contains sync-telemetry.analytics-cdn.net' matched 21 DNS query packets.

The DNS tunneling Python script (setup_task.sh line 367) explicitly binds to src_ip='10.0.1.10'. All DNS tunnel queries originate from this IP. Verified during live testing: tshark extraction of ip.src for DNS tunnel queries all showed 10.0.1.10.

Independently computed by running hashlib.sha256() on the exact CSV heredoc content from setup_task.sh lines 23-37. The CSV is 775 bytes, 13 lines (1 header + 12 data rows). Round-trip verified: CSV -> XOR(key=k7Xm9pQ2) -> base64 -> URL-safe -> split into 21 chunks -> reassemble -> URL-unsafe -> base64 decode -> XOR decrypt -> SHA-256 matches. Also verified end-to-end inside the live VM during testing.

First line of the CSV heredoc in setup_task.sh line 24. Independently verified by reading the heredoc content and splitting on newline. This is the CSV header row with 6 columns: id, first_name, last_name, email, ssn, department.

The CSV is 775 bytes. After XOR encryption and base64 encoding, the URL-safe encoded string is 1034 characters. Split into 50-char chunks yields ceil(1034/50) = 21 chunks, so 21 DNS queries are sent. Verified independently by running the encode logic locally (got 21 chunks) and during live testing via tshark count of DNS queries containing the exfil domain.

Hardcoded in setup_task.sh line 42 (XOR_KEY variable), in the Python encode script (line 59), and in the C2 server response JSON. The key is 8 bytes. Verified that XOR with this key is reversible (round-trip test passed).

DNS tunneling script at setup_task.sh line 372: query_name = f'{i:03d}.{chunk}.{domain}'. Sequence numbers are zero-padded 3-digit integers (000, 001, ..., 020). Chunks use URL-safe base64 (+ replaced with -, / replaced with _, = stripped). Verified during live testing by examining DNS query names in tshark output.
Loopback aliases configured in setup_task.sh lines 100-105. /etc/hosts entries at lines 110-116. All verified via live testing (ip addr show lo showed all aliases).

Verified during live testing: tshark -r corporate_traffic.pcapng | wc -l returned 167. This includes ~100 TCP/TLS packets for 7 HTTPS sessions, ~26 DNS packets (13 queries + 13 responses for normal DNS), ~42 DNS packets (21 queries + 21 responses for tunneling), plus some TCP RST/FIN packets.

Setup script makes 7 curl requests: 3 in phase 1 (cdn.jquery.com, api.github.com, fonts.googleapis.com), 2 in phase 3 (cdn.jquery.com again, api.github.com again), 1 C2 in phase 4 (secure-updates.corp-cdn.net), 1 in phase 7 (fonts.googleapis.com again). Verified via live testing: tshark SNI extraction showed 4 unique SNIs with 7 total Client Hello packets.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic forensic investigation task. The capture at ~/Documents/captures/corporate_traffic.pcapng contains approximately 167 packets of mixed legitimate and malicious traffic on loopback aliases. A TLS session key log at ~/Documents/captures/tls_keys.log enables decryption of all 7 HTTPS sessions across 4 servers. The C2 server has SNI 'secure-updates.corp-cdn.net' (IP 10.0.1.20). After TLS decryption, the C2 HTTP response body contains the exact JSON: {"action": "exfiltrate", "method": "dns_tunnel", "domain": "sync-telemetry.analytics-cdn.net", "xor_key": "k7Xm9pQ2", "encoding": "base64"}. Three legitimate HTTPS servers are cdn.jquery.com (10.0.1.30), api.github.com (10.0.1.40), and fonts.googleapis.com (10.0.1.50). DNS tunneling queries go to domain 'sync-telemetry.analytics-cdn.net' from source IP 10.0.1.10, totaling 21 queries. Each query has format <3-digit-seq>.<url-safe-base64-chunk>.<domain> (e.g., '000.<chunk>.sync-telemetry.analytics-cdn.net'). The encoded data uses URL-safe base64 where + is replaced with -, / with _, and = padding is stripped. Before base64, the data is XOR-encrypted with key 'k7Xm9pQ2'. To decode: extract subdomain labels, sort by sequence number, concatenate, reverse URL-safe chars (- to +, _ to /), add base64 padding, base64 decode, XOR decrypt with key. The decoded data is a 775-byte CSV with header 'id,first_name,last_name,email,ssn,department' and 12 employee records. Its SHA-256 hash is 5d27337ca43271af60a42bf93491e63cec9714c805eb78cc02347c3a8c154baa. Normal DNS queries (13 total) go to domains like google.com, weather.com, stackoverflow.com, etc. All traffic originates from 10.0.1.10. The DNS server at 10.0.1.60 responds to all queries with IP 10.0.1.60.
